title: delphinium parryi subsp. eastwoodiae
intro: Delphinium parryi subsp. eastwoodiae, also known as Eastwood's delphinium, is a subspecies of the Delphinium parryi plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the buttercup family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Delphinium parryi subsp. eastwoodiae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. Delphinium parryi subsp. eastwoodiae can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve for topical application, or as a decoction for internal use, although caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
